characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A young man who discovered at an early age that he possessed the power to bring back the dead, but only for a minute, or something else in close proximity would die in return. After realizing — through his resurrected mother kissing him goodnight — that a single touch would kill them forever, Ned is shipped off to boarding school by his absentee father, separating him from his childhood sweetheart Chuck. Some years later, Ned has become a piemaker who makes money on the side by partnering with Emerson Cod, a private investigator, in resurrecting the victims of difficult murders in order to track down their killers. He lives a smooth, if emotionally detached life, until he learns of Chuck's murder and inadvertently lets her live longer than her intended one minute. Along with Emerson, occasionally Olive, and definitely Digby, they solve crimes. Arbitrary Skepticism: He raises the dead, but doesn't believe in ghosts. Bad Liar: He's an absolutelyterribleliar who can't sustain even the simplest of deceptions. Balancing Death's Books: How his powers work. If someone he brings back is alive for more than a minute, someone else dies in their place. The exact target is usually random and it seems only Ned himself is immune to it and it seems to work on a proportional basis, meaning an animal kept alive would result in another animal dying.

characters.name: Ned
characters.description: A young man who discovered at an early age that he possessed the power to bring back the dead, but only for a minute, or something else in close proximity would die in return. After realizing — through his resurrected mother kissing him goodnight — that a single touch would kill them forever, Ned is shipped off to boarding school by his absentee father, separating him from his childhood sweetheart Chuck. Some years later, Ned has become a piemaker who makes money on the side by partnering with Emerson Cod, a private investigator, in resurrecting the victims of difficult murders in order to track down their killers. He lives a smooth, if emotionally detached life, until he learns of Chuck's murder and inadvertently lets her live longer than her intended one minute. Along with Emerson, occasionally Olive, and definitely Digby, they solve crimes. Arbitrary Skepticism: He raises the dead, but doesn't believe in ghosts. Bad Liar: He's an absolutelyterribleliar who can't sustain even the simplest of deceptions. Balancing Death's Books: How his powers work. If someone he brings back is alive for more than a minute, someone else dies in their place. The exact target is usually random and it seems only Ned himself is immune to it and it seems to work on a proportional basis, meaning an animal kept alive would result in another animal dying.

characters.name: Chuck Charles
characters.description: Ned's childhood sweetheart. Upon the death of her father, who had died to balance out the revival of Ned's mother, she is sent to live with her aunts Lily and Vivian. She and Ned do not meet again until he is called upon to solve her murder on a cruise ship, upon which he accidentally revives her (killing the funeral home owner in return). Despite being legally dead, Chuck manages to reintegrate herself into Ned's life, rekindling their romance along the way, but on one condition — they can't ever touch, or else Chuck dies again forever. Her kind, free-spirited nature provide good faith and cheer to the show's dynamics, something Ned is appreciative of. Alliterative Name:Charlotte "Chuck"Charles. Attractive Zombie: She is as beautiful as she is when she was alive. Badass Bookworm: She reads a lot and is capable of defending herself against killers. Beauty Is Never Tarnished: Granted the funeral home certainly tidied her up for her burial, but once Chuck's been revived she looksverygood for someone who was first asphyxiated and then dumped in the ocean, especially since Ned's power doesn't necessarily heal someone when he revives them.

characters.name: Emerson Cod
characters.description: A private investigator who frequently partners with Ned. Dour and sour, he provides some of the show's more down-to-earth comments. He's alsoa very avid knitter, using the hobby as a stress relief. The Big Guy: Being the tallest member and armed member of the crew. Born Detective: His mother was a detective to and he inherited her skills and desire for it. Catchphrase: "Oh, HELL no!" The Cobbler's Children Have No Shoes: Even without Ned's aid Emerson Cod is a capable investigator. But he still can't find his daughter.

characters.name: Olive Snook
characters.description: Tiny, adorable Olive is Ned's neighbor, a waitress at the Pie Hole, and unfortunately hopelessly in love with Ned. Despite her initial dislike for Chuck, the latter eventually warms herself to her. At the same time, Olive strikes up a friendship with Chuck's aunts. Beware the Nice Ones: She's very sweet until you set her temper off. Big Eater: Is a stress eater to the tune of six whole pies in one sitting. Clingy Jealous Girl: Is initially very unhappy with Ned and Chuck's relationship, butCharacter Developmentsees her get past this. Competition Freak: She takes cooking competitionsextremelyseriously.

characters.name: Vivian Charles
characters.description: One of Chuck's aunts, who is the softer, kinder one of the two. She and Lily were once the famous synchronized swimming duo Darling Mermaid Darlings. All Girls Want Bad Boys:She is more intrigued than disturbed by the fact that Dwight Dixon spent 22 years in prison.Although we'll never know for sure (alas!) Charles Charles, her one-time fiance, was a friend of Dwight's and most likely had several deep, dark secrets too. She is more intrigued than disturbed by the fact that Dwight Dixon spent 22 years in prison. Although we'll never know for sure (alas!) Charles Charles, her one-time fiance, was a friend of Dwight's and most likely had several deep, dark secrets too. Beware the Nice Ones:When she finds out about Lily's infidelity with Charles Charles, Vivian responds by hurling a decorative plate at Lily and kicking her out of their home.

characters.name: Lily Charles
characters.description: The other of Chuck's aunts, who is colder and snarkier than her sister. She and Vivian were once the famous synchronized swimming duo Darling Mermaid Darlings. Afraid of Clowns: She has severe coulrophobia, even the sight of a toy clown sends her into hysterics. According to Vivian it was caused by an incident in their childhood where their grandfather chased her around with a toy clown. Big Sister Instinct: She's slightly older than Vivian and had it drilled into her as a child to protect her younger sister after it was discovered Vivian has a heart condition.It's why she kept her affair with Charles and resulting pregnancy a secret, and never told anyone that she was Chuck's mother. Cool Old Lady: An eyepatch wearing, retired athlete who takes down a killer. Deadpan Snarker: Tends to have a very bitter, sarcastic sense of humor.

characters.name: Digby
characters.description: Ned's beloved childhood golden retriever. When Ned was nine, Digby was run over by an eighteen-wheeler. Ned's sorrow at this led to him discovering his mysterious power. In the present day, Digby still lives with Ned, but is frequently cared for by Olive. The Ageless: Hasn't aged since Ned resurrected him during the opening scene of "Pie-lette". He's still alive in the present, well beyond the usual age range for a Golden Retriever, and hasn't aged a day, raising the unanswered question as to whether Ned's powers also stop or at least considerably slow the aging process for humans as well. Back from the Dead: Got run over, then brought back. Canine Companion: Has been Ned's dog sidekick since childhood.

characters.name: Recurring Characters
characters.description: The coroner at the morgue where most of thevictims of the weekend up. He's more than a little suspicious of Ned and Chuck, and how Emerson keeps bringing them along on investigations. Deadpan Snarker: He doesn't know nor care why Ned is being brought along and obviously know he is not whatever expert he is posing as at the moment. He just take the bribe and ask questions just to mess with them. No Name Given: We never find out his name. ATraveling Salesmanwho treats his own fear that Earth's atmosphere will get sucked away through various homeopathic remedies. He gives Chuck antidepressants that she bakes into her aunts' pies. He's also in love with Olive. Alliterative Name:AlfredoAldarisio.

characters.name: Family Members
characters.description: Ned's unnamed mother, the first person to be resurrected from his gift at the cost of Chuck's father. She died again upon kissing him goodnight. Good Parents: She doesn't have much screentime, but she was clearly a good and loving parent to Ned. She's also shown comforting young Chuck in the aftermath of Charles Charles' death. Missing Mom: She died from a brain anuerysm. Ned revived her, but the next time she touched him she died for good. Supreme Chef: She was an excellent piemaker; Ned was inspired by her. Through His Stomach: According to Dwight Dixon, her piemaking skills went a long way in making Ned's father fall in love with her.

characters.name: Oneshot Characters
characters.description: From "Dummy." An automobile safety specialist at the Dandy Lion Worldwide Industries. Dying Declaration of Love/Last Request: Upon his resurrection his last request is for the team to tell Jeanine he loved her. Killed to Uphold the Masquerade:He was murdered to keep him from exposing a fatal flaw in the Dandy Lion's design. Mistaken for Cheating: By Jeanine, due to his increasingly long hours at work. Nice Guy: Apparently, he collected butterflies but always set them free afterwards.
